title: Watts Nickel Electroplating Bath Quality Controller
description: Closed-loop electroplating controller that monitors bath pH, current density, and temperature, adjusts galvanostatic current to maintain deposit quality within the Hull-cell optimum window, displays status on LCD, and triggers fault isolation on out-of-window conditions.
objective: Hold current density 2–5 A/dm², pH 3.5–4.5, temperature 45–55 °C; log bath state every 5 s to serial; fault-cut current and alert on out-of-spec condition.
constraints: Arduino Mega; LM317 current source; pH electrode; NTC bath probe; LCD display; BOM < $50.
supporting_fact_titles: Electroplating Current Density and Hull Cell Deposit Quality
element_usage_notes: pH reading used for fault threshold and operator display
Current setpoint updated each loop cycle; feedback from INA169 shunt monitor
Temperature monitored for process window compliance; no active heating in this design
Shows live pH, current, temp and fault code on two lines

element_interactions.description: pH out-of-spec triggers current cutoff via fault handler
Measured current displayed on LCD line 1
Bath temperature displayed on LCD line 2
pH value co-displayed for operator monitoring
